Its use is further discussed in </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Management&#8217;s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition &#8211; Results of Operations &#8211; Operating Results Table</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:bottom;padding-left:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;padding-right:2px;"><div style="overflow:hidden;height:5px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:bottom;padding-left:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;padding-right:2px;"><div style="overflow:hidden;height:5px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;</font></div></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top;padding-left:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;padding-right:2px;"><div style="text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">NYMEX</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;padding-left:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;padding-right:2px;"><div style="text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">The New York Mercantile Exchange.  In the context of our oil and natural gas sales, NYMEX pricing represents the West Texas Intermediate benchmark price for crude oil and Henry Hub benchmark price for natural reserves, are as likely as not to be recovered.</font></div></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:bottom;padding-left:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;padding-right:2px;"><div present value using an annual discount rate of 10%. PV-10 Values were prepared using average hydrocarbon prices equal to the unweighted arithmetic average of hydrocarbon prices on the first day of each month within the 12-month period preceding the reporting date.&#160;&#160;PV-10 Value is a non-GAAP measure and its use is further discussed in footnote 4 to the table included in Item 1, </font><font In the context of our oil and natural gas production, tertiary recovery is also referred to as EOR.</font></div><div style="text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div></td></tr></table></div></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">* This definition is an abbreviated version of the complete definition set forth in Rule&#160;4-10(a) of Regulation S-X. Business and Properties</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div recovery utilizing CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font acquired properties through a combination of exploitation, drilling and proven engineering extraction practices, with the most significant emphasis relating to tertiary recovery operations.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div and, as the context may require, its subsidiaries.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">2012 AND 2013 MAJOR PROPERTY EXCHANGES AND ACQUISITIONS</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">We set the stage for our 2013 business developments with two major transactions.  In December 2012, we closed a sale and exchange transaction with Exxon Mobil Corporation and its wholly-owned subsidiary XTO Energy Inc, (collectively, "ExxonMobil") under which we sold to ExxonMobil our Bakken area assets in North Dakota and Montana in exchange for (1) $1.3 billion in cash, (2) operating interests in Hartzog Draw and Webster fields in Wyoming and Texas, respectively, and (3) an overriding royalty interest equivalent to an approximate one-third ownership interest in ExxonMobil&#8217;s CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves in LaBarge Field in Wyoming (the "Bakken Exchange Transaction").  We utilized cash received in this exchange to fund our March 2013 acquisition of producing assets in the Cedar Creek Anticline ("CCA") in Montana and North Dakota from ConocoPhillips Company ("ConocoPhillips") for $1.05 billion in cash, before closing adjustments.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Taken together, these two asset transactions nearly replaced the production of the sold assets with production from the acquired assets, exchanged proved reserves with a high proved undeveloped component in the Bakken for reserves that were nearly all proved developed in CCA, increased our Rocky Mountain CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves by 1.3 Tcf and our CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;deliverability by up to 115 MMcf/d, and positioned us to provide dividends to our stockholders as discussed below.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">- </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;5</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;-</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><a style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;" href="#sAB171F94BB68041C84745A8C8894EBF7"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Table of Contents</font></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Denbury Resources Inc.</font></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">&#32;BUSINESS DEVELOPMENTS</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">In the fourth quarter of 2013, following a comprehensive review of our long-term plans, we announced our intention to expand our shareholder value proposition to include both growth and income.  The expansion includes the initiation of regular quarterly cash dividend payments to our shareholders starting with $0.0625 per share (a rate of $0.25 per share on an annualized basis).  The first quarterly cash dividend of $0.0625 was declared on January 28, 2014, payable March 25, 2014, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on February 25, 2014.  Based on our current financial projections and commodity price outlook, we expect to grow our annual dividend rate to between $0.50 per share and $0.60 per share in 2015 and at a sustainable rate thereafter.  All dividends are discretionary and subject to declaration by Denbury&#8217;s Board of Directors.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">To expand our free cash flow, we adjusted certain of our development plans and timelines for various capital projects, principally in the Rocky Mountain region, in order to reduce our spending on certain major infrastructure projects over the next few years.  These adjustments allowed us to accelerate our plan of providing a return to our shareholders through a growing cash dividend, while still growing our reserves and production.  Our focused strategy, significant inventory of development projects and proven track record of value creation give us confidence that we can deliver a long-term cash flow profile that is unique among independent oil companies and successfully execute on our value-driven growth and income strategy in 2014 and beyond.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;business developments also include the following:</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:24px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:0px;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8226;</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Increased our average tertiary oil production to </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">38,477</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bbls/d in </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, a </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">9%</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">increase</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from average tertiary production in </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, primarily due to continued field development and expansion of facilities in our existing CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2 </sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">floods at Delhi, Hastings, Heidelberg and Oyster Bayou fields.</font></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="font-family:Times New Roman; The net proceeds of approximately $1.18 billion were used to repurchase or redeem our 9&#189;% Senior Subordinated Notes due 2016 and our 9&#190;% Senior Subordinated Notes due 2016, and to pay down a portion of outstanding borrowings on our bank credit facility.</font></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;padding-left:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:24px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:0px;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8226;</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Closed our acquisition of producing assets in the CCA in Montana and North Dakota in March 2013 from a wholly-owned subsidiary of ConocoPhillips for $1.05 billion in cash, before closing adjustments.  The assets purchased include both additional interests in certain of our existing operated fields in CCA, as well as operating interests in other CCA fields.</font></div></td></tr></table><br><div href="#sAB171F94BB68041C84745A8C8894EBF7"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Table of Contents</font></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">OIL AND NATURAL GAS OPERATIONS</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Summary.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Our oil and natural gas properties are concentrated in the Gulf Coast and Rocky Mountain regions of the United States.&#160;&#160;Currently our properties with proved and producing reserves in the Gulf Coast region are situated in Mississippi, Texas, Louisiana and Alabama, and in the Rocky Mountain region in Montana, North Dakota and Wyoming.&#160;&#160;Our primary focus is using CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in EOR, and we expect the development plan for our current portfolio of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects will allow us to grow our oil production for the remainder of the decade.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">We have been conducting and expanding EOR operations on our assets in the Gulf Coast region since 1999, and as a result, we currently have many more CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects in this region than in the Rocky Mountain region.  In the Gulf Coast region, we own what is, to our knowledge, its only significant naturally occurring source of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  These large volumes of naturally occurring CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;have allowed us to significantly grow our production in that region.  In addition to the sources of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;we currently own, in 2013 we began to purchase and use anthropogenic (man-made) CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in our tertiary operations.  We believe these man-made sources of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2 </sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">will help us recover additional oil from mature oil fields while also providing an economical way to reduce atmospheric CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2 </sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">emissions through the concurrent underground storage of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from our oil-producing EOR operations, and expect the amount of anthropogenic CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;we use in such operations to grow in the future.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Through </font><font costs and amounts assigned to goodwill), have recovered all of these costs, and have generated </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$1.5 billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of excess net cash flow (revenue less operating expenses and capital expenditures, excluding capital expenditures related to pipelines and CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;source fields).&#160;&#160;Of this total invested amount, approximately $206.7&#160;million (6%) has been spent on fields that did not yet have any appreciable proved reserves at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;The proved oil reserves in our Gulf Coast tertiary oil</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:7pt;">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">fields have a year-end </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;PV-10 Value of </font><font currently serves our Hastings and Oyster Bayou fields, we have invested a total of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$2.1 billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">-producing assets and pipelines in the Gulf Coast region.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">We began operations in the Rocky Mountain region in 2010 as part of our merger with Encore Acquisition Company ("Encore").&#160;&#160;In late 2012, we completed construction of the first section of the 20-inch Greencore Pipeline, our first CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline in the Rocky Mountain region, and received our first CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;deliveries from the Lost Cabin gas plant in central Wyoming during the first quarter of 2013.  We also began injecting CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;into Grieve Field in Wyoming early in 2013 and currently expect initial tertiary oil production from Grieve Field in 2015.&#160; We started injections at our Bell Creek Field in Montana during the second quarter of 2013, with tertiary oil production from this field commencing in the third quarter of 2013.  In addition to our current tertiary floods in the Rocky Mountain region, we currently have long-term plans to flood Hartzog Draw Field and CCA after we perform additional non-tertiary development of these fields.  CCA is a geological structure over 126 miles in length consisting of 14 different operating areas.  Our Riley Ridge Field acquisitions in 2010 and 2011 and acquisition of an interest in CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves from ExxonMobil in 2012 are expected to provide us the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;necessary for our current inventory of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects in the Rocky Mountain region.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Field Summary Table.</font><font reserve quantities and the associated PV-10 Value of those reserves as of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font were prepared by DeGolyer and MacNaughton, independent petroleum engineers located in Dallas, Texas.&#160;&#160;We serve as operator of virtually all of our significant properties, in which we also own most of the interests, although typically less than a 100% working interest, and a lesser NRI due to royalties and other burdens.&#160;&#160;For additional oil and natural gas reserves style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">The Cedar Creek Anticline consists of a series of 14 different operating areas.</font></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;padding-left:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Enhanced Oil Recovery Overview</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;&#32;CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;used in EOR is one of the most efficient tertiary recovery mechanisms for producing crude oil.&#160;&#160;When injected at pressure into underground, oil-bearing rock formations, CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;acts somewhat like a solvent as it travels through the reservoir rock, mixing with and modifying the characteristics of the oil so it can be produced and sold.&#160;&#160;CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;tertiary floods are unique in that they require large volumes of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  The terms "tertiary flood," "CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;flood" and "CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR" are used interchangeably throughout this document.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">While enhanced oil recovery projects utilizing CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;have been successfully performed by numerous oil and gas companies in a wide range of oil-bearing reservoirs in different oil-producing basins, we apply what we have learned and developed over the years to fields to improve and increase sweep efficiency within the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects we operate, which include (1) well evaluation and monitoring methods, (2) monitoring the flood and striving to direct the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;to all economically recoverable portions of the oil-bearing reservoirs, (3) new completion techniques, (4) varied operating equipment and operating methods, and (5) application of intense reservoir management and production techniques.&#160;&#160;We began our CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;operations in August 1999, when we acquired Little Creek Field, followed by our acquisition of Jackson Dome CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves and the NEJD pipeline in 2001.&#160;&#160;Based upon our success at Little Creek and the ownership of the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves, we began to transition our capital spending and acquisition efforts to focus a greater percentage on CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR and, over time, transformed our strategy to focus primarily, and now almost exclusively, on owning and operating oil fields that are well suited for CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects, although prior to tertiary flooding, we strive to maximize the currently sizeable primary and secondary production from our prospective tertiary fields and from fields in which tertiary floods have commenced but still contain significant non-tertiary production.&#160;&#160;With the sale of our Bakken area assets in 2012, our asset base today almost entirely consists of, or otherwise relates to, oil fields that we are currently flooding with CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;or plan to flood with CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in the future, or assets that produce CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;We believe our investments, experience and acquired knowledge give us a strategic and competitive advantage in the areas in which we operate.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Our tertiary operations have grown so that (1) </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">49%</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of our proved reserves at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;are proved tertiary oil reserves; (2) </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">55%</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of our </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;production was related to tertiary oil operations (on a BOE basis); and (3) </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">77%</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of our </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;capital expenditures (excluding acquisitions) were related to our tertiary oil operations.&#160;&#160;At year-end </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, the proved oil reserves in our tertiary recovery oil fields had an estimated PV-10 Value of approximately </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$6.9 billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, or </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">64%</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of our total PV-10 Value.&#160;&#160;In addition, there are significant probable and possible reserves at several other fields for which tertiary operations are underway or planned.&#160;&#160;Although the up-front cost of tertiary production infrastructure and time to construct these pipelines and production facilities is greater than in primary oil recovery, we believe tertiary recovery has several favorable, offsetting and unique attributes, including (1) a lower exploration risk, as we are operating oil fields that have significant historical production and reservoir and geological data, (2) a reasonable rate of return at relatively low oil prices (we currently estimate our economic break-even point before corporate-related overhead, based on currently estimated expenses, occurs at oil prices in the low-to-mid $40-per-barrel range, depending on the specific field and area), (3) limited competition for this recovery method in our geographic regions, (4) our EOR operations are generally less disruptive to new habitats in comparison to other oil and natural gas development because we further develop existing (as opposed to new) oil fields, and (5) through our oil-producing EOR operations, we concurrently store anthropogenic CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2 </sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">in the same underground formations that had previously trapped and stored oil and natural gas.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Tertiary Oil Properties</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Gulf Coast Region</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:7pt;font-style:italic;">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Sources and Pipelines</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Jackson Dome.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">&#160; </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Our primary Gulf Coast CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;source, Jackson Dome, located near Jackson, Mississippi, was discovered during the 1970s by oil and gas companies that were exploring for hydrocarbons.&#160;&#160;This large and relatively pure source of naturally occurring CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;(98% CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">) is, to our knowledge, the only significant underground deposit of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in the United States east of the Mississippi River, and we believe that it, together with the related CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline infrastructure, provides us a significant strategic advantage in the acquisition of other properties in Mississippi, Louisiana and southeastern Texas that are well suited for CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">- </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;9</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;-</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><a style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;" href="#sAB171F94BB68041C84745A8C8894EBF7"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Table of Contents</font></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Denbury Resources Inc.</font></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">We acquired Jackson Dome in February 2001 in a purchase that also gave us ownership and control of the NEJD CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline and provided us with a reliable supply of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;at a reasonable and predictable cost for our Gulf Coast CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;tertiary recovery operations.&#160;&#160;Since February 2001, we have acquired and drilled numerous CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">-producing wells, significantly increasing our estimated proved Gulf Coast CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves from approximately 800 Bcf at the time of acquisition to approximately </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">6.1</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Tcf as of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;The CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserve estimates are based on a gross working interest of the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves, of which our net revenue interest is approximately </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">4.8 Tcf</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, and is included in the evaluation of proved CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves prepared by our outside reserves engineer, DeGolyer and MacNaughton.&#160;&#160;In discussing our available CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves, we make reference to the gross amount of proved and probable reserves, as this is the amount that is available both for our own tertiary recovery programs and for industrial users who are customers of Denbury and others, as we are responsible for distributing the entire CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;production stream.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">In addition to the proved reserves, we estimate that we have 2.5 Tcf of probable CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves at Jackson Dome.&#160;&#160;The majority of our probable reserves at Jackson Dome are located in structures that have been drilled and tested in the area but are not currently capable of producing or are not considered proved reserves because (1) the original well is plugged; (2) they are located in fault blocks that are immediately adjacent to fault blocks with proved reserves; (3) they are in undrilled structures where we have sufficient subsurface data, and seismic and geophysical attributes that provide a high degree of certainty that CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;is present; or (4) they are reserves associated with increasing the ultimate recovery factor from our existing reservoirs with proved reserves.&#160;&#160;Our historically high drilling success rate, coupled with our seismic data across the undrilled structures, provide us with a reasonably high degree of certainty that additional proved CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves will be discovered and developed.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Although our current proved CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves are quite large, in order to continue our tertiary development of oil fields in the Gulf Coast region, incremental deliverability of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;is required.&#160;&#160;In order to obtain additional CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;deliverability, we have conducted several 3D seismic surveys in the Jackson Dome area over the past several years, and anticipate drilling one development well in </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;that is intended to increase the area's productive capacity.  </font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">In addition to our drilling at Jackson Dome, we continue to expand our processing and dehydration capacities, and we continue to install pipelines and/or pumping stations necessary to transport the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;through our controlled pipeline network.  We expect our current proved reserves of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, coupled with a risked drilling program at Jackson Dome and expected anthropogenic sources, to provide sufficient quantities of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;for us to develop our proved and probable EOR reserves in the Gulf Coast region.  Additionally, in the future, we believe that once a CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;flood in a field reaches its productive economic limit, we could recycle a portion of any CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;that remains in that reservoir and utilize it for oil production in another tertiary flood.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">In the Gulf Coast region, we also currently sell CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;to third-party industrial users under contracts of various terms and currently have three CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;volumetric production payment contracts.&#160;&#160;Approximately 91% of our average daily CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;produced or acquired from anthropogenic sources in </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;and </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2011</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;was used in our tertiary recovery operations, with the balance delivered to third-party industrial users.&#160;&#160;During </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we used an average of 913 MMcf/d of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;(including CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from anthropogenic sources) for our tertiary activities.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Gulf Coast Anthropogenic CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Sources.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;In addition to our natural source of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we are currently party to four long-term contracts to purchase man-made CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from four plants.&#160;&#160;We currently purchase anthropogenic CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from an industrial facility in Port Arthur, Texas and from a plant in Geismar, Louisiana, and we anticipate taking deliveries in late 2014 from Mississippi Power's Kemper County Energy Facility.  We estimate these three sources will supply, in the aggregate, approximately 185 MMcf/d of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;to our EOR operations, although under certain circumstances they could provide higher or lower volumes.&#160;&#160;If the fourth plant for which we have a long-term CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;purchase contract were also to be built (targeted for the 2018 time frame), we currently estimate this source in Lake Charles, Louisiana could potentially add another 200 MMcf/d of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;volumes to our anthropogenic sources.&#160;&#160;Construction of this remaining plant is considered probable, although such construction is contingent on the satisfactory resolution of various matters, including financing.&#160;&#160;Additionally, we are in ongoing discussions with other parties who have plans to construct plants near the Green Pipeline.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">In addition to the potential CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;sources discussed above, we continue to have ongoing discussions with owners of existing plants of various types that emit CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;that we may be able to purchase and/or transport.  In order to capture such volumes, we (or the plant owner) would need to install additional equipment, which includes, at a minimum, compression and dehydration facilities.&#160;&#160;Most of these existing plants emit relatively small volumes of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, generally less than our contracted sources, but such volumes may still be attractive if the source is located near CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipelines.&#160;&#160;The capture of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;could also be influenced by potential </font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">- </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;10</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;-</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><a style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;" href="#sAB171F94BB68041C84745A8C8894EBF7"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Table of Contents</font></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Denbury Resources Inc.</font></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">federal legislation, which could impose economic penalties for atmospheric CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2 </sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">emissions.&#160;&#160;We believe that we are a likely purchaser of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;captured in our areas of operation because of the scale of our tertiary operations and our CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline infrastructure. </font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Gulf Coast CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Pipelines.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;&#32;We acquired the 183-mile NEJD CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline that runs from Jackson Dome to near Donaldsonville, Louisiana, as part of the 2001 acquisition of our Jackson Dome source.&#160;&#160;Since 2001 we have acquired or constructed nearly 750 miles of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipelines, which give us the ability to deliver CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;throughout the Gulf Coast region.&#160;&#160;As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we have access to over 940 miles of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipelines in the Gulf Coast region.  In addition to the NEJD CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2 </sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">pipeline, the major pipelines are the Free State Pipeline (90 miles), the Delta Pipeline (110 miles), the Green Pipeline Texas (120 miles), and the Green Pipeline Louisiana (200 miles).</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Completion of the Green Pipeline allowed for the first CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injection into Hastings Field, located near Houston, Texas, in 2010, and gives us the ability to deliver CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;to oil fields all along the Gulf Coast from Baton Rouge, Louisiana, to Alvin, Texas.&#160;&#160;At the present time, most of the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;flowing in the Green Pipeline is delivered from the Jackson Dome area, but we began receiving anthropogenic CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from an industrial facility in Port Arthur, Texas in 2012, and are currently transporting a third party's CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;for a fee to the sales point at Hastings Field.&#160; We expect the volume of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;transported through the Green Pipeline to increase in future years as we develop our inventory of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects in the Houston area.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Tertiary Properties with Tertiary Production and Proved Tertiary Reserves at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Mature properties.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;&#32;Mature properties include our longest-producing properties which are generally located along our NEJD CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline in southwest Mississippi and Louisiana and our Free State pipeline in east Mississippi.&#160;&#160;This group of properties includes our initial CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;field, Little Creek, as well as several other fields (Brookhaven, Cranfield, Eucutta, Lockhart Crossing, Mallalieu, Martinville, McComb and Soso fields).&#160;&#160;These fields accounted for </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">36%</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of our total </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR production and approximately </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">21%</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of our year-end proved tertiary reserves.&#160;&#160;These fields have been producing for some time, and their production is generally declining.  Many of these fields contain multiple reservoirs that are amenable to CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR.  In </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $115 million to further develop our mature tertiary properties.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">In order to improve the oil recovery of our more mature CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects, we have experimented with various techniques such as cement squeezes (injection and producing wells), chemical squeezes, perforation design, mechanical isolation assemblies and operating pressure controls.&#160;&#160;We have also utilized water-alternating-gas injections, where water is substituted for the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;for a given volume and then CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;is injected behind the water.  Each one of these processes has had some success, and we plan to continue to utilize them in the future where appropriate.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">From the time we originally acquired these properties through </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we have </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">recovered all our costs</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;relating to our mature properties, and the excess net cash flow (revenue less operating expenses and capital expenditures, including the acquisition costs) from the mature properties through that date was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$1.9 billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, the estimated PV-10 Value of our mature properties was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">$1.6 billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Delhi Field.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;&#32;Delhi Field is located east of Monroe, Louisiana.&#160;&#160;During May 2006, we purchased Delhi for $50 million, plus an approximate 25% reversionary interest to the seller after we receive $200 million in "total net cash flow," as defined.&#160;&#160;We began well and facility development in 2008 and began delivering CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;to the field in the fourth quarter of 2009 via the Delta Pipeline, which runs from Tinsley Field to Delhi Field.&#160;&#160;First tertiary production occurred at Delhi Field in the first quarter of 2010.&#160;&#160;Production from Delhi in the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;averaged </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">4,793</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bbls/d, down from 5,237 Bbls/d in the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;This decline in production is primarily related to our efforts to remediate a release of well fluids within an area of Delhi Field in the second quarter of 2013, consisting of a mixture of carbon dioxide, saltwater, natural gas and oil.  During 2013, we recorded </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$114.0 million</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of lease operating expenses in our Consolidated Statement of Operations related to this incident.  Costs incurred as a result of the release, together with lower production levels, are currently expected to delay the effective date of the reversionary interest into 2014, the specific timing of which is dependent upon, among other things, the amount and timing of any potential insurance proceeds received and their application to the calculation of "total net cash flow," as well as oil prices, production, and production costs.  We currently estimate that the reversionary date could occur as late as the fourth quarter of 2014.  See Item 7, </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8211;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">&#32;Overview </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8211;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">&#32;Delhi Field Release </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">and </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;">Note 11</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:italic;font-weight:normal;">Commitments and Contingencies</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;to the Consolidated Financial Statements for further discussion of this matter.  In </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $40 million in this field, primarily to install a natural gas liquids extraction plant, which we anticipate will be operational in 2015.</font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div We began producing oil from our EOR operations at Hastings Field in the first quarter of 2012, and we booked initial proved tertiary reserves for the West Hastings Unit in 2012.&#160;&#160;During the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, tertiary production from Hastings Field averaged </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">4,270</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bbls/d, compared to 3,409 Bbls/d in the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  In </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $75 million to continue developing the West Hastings Unit, including the development of additional patterns and expansion of the processing facilities.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div (revenue less operating expenses and capital expenditures, including the acquisition cost) from Hastings Field was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$336 million</font><font billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">. </font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Heidelberg Field.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;Heidelberg Field is located in Mississippi and consists of an East Unit and a West Unit.&#160;&#160;Construction of the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;facility, connecting pipeline and well work commenced on the West Heidelberg Unit during 2008, with our first CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections into the Eutaw zone in the fourth quarter of 2008.&#160;&#160;Our first tertiary oil production occurred in the second quarter of 2009, and during 2010, we added injection patterns and expanded the central processing facility.  In 2013, we began flooding the Christmas zone.&#160;&#160;During the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;In </font><font Heidelberg Units, including an expansion of our development of the Eutaw and Christmas zones and adjustments to our CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;floods of existing zones to better direct the CO</font><font optimize oil recovery from the field.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font Field, and the remaining investment to be recovered (revenue less operating expenses and capital expenditures, including the acquisition costs) from the field was </font><font
