Field was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">$1.1 billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Oyster Bayou Field.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;We acquired a majority interest in Oyster Bayou Field in 2007.  The field is located in southeast Texas, east of Galveston Bay.&#160;&#160;We began CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections into Oyster Bayou in the second quarter of 2010.&#160;&#160;Oyster Bayou Field is somewhat unique when compared to our other CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR projects because the field covers a relatively small area of 3,912 acres. &#160;We commenced tertiary production from Oyster Bayou Field in the fourth quarter of 2011 from the Frio A-1 zone and booked initial proved tertiary reserves for the field in 2012.&#160;&#160;During the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, tertiary production at Oyster Bayou Field averaged </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">3,869</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bbls/d, compared to 1,826 Bbls/d in the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  In </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $50 million to develop the Frio A-2 zone and optimize our Frio A-1 zone development.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">From inception through </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we had </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">not yet recovered</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;our investment in this field, and the remaining investment to be recovered (revenue less operating expenses and capital expenditures, including the acquisition costs) from Oyster Bayou Field was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$98 million</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, the estimated PV-10 Value of Oyster Bayou Field was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">$550.0 million</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Tinsley Field.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;We acquired Tinsley Field in 2006.  The field is located in Mississippi, was discovered and first developed in the 1930s and is separated into different fault blocks.&#160;&#160;As is the case with the majority of fields in Mississippi, Tinsley produces from multiple reservoirs.&#160;&#160;Our CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;enhanced oil recovery operations at Tinsley have thus far targeted the Woodruff formation, although there is additional potential in the Perry sandstone and other smaller reservoirs.&#160;&#160;We commenced tertiary oil production from Tinsley Field in the second quarter of 2008.&#160; In 2014, we expect to invest approximately $50 million to continue our development of the North Fault Block and to develop dedicated injection wells in the East Fault Block.&#160;&#160;We currently expect our development of the Woodruff to be substantially complete by the end of 2014.  During the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, the average tertiary oil production was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">7,809</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bbls/d, compared to 8,166 Bbls/d in the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">- </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;12</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;-</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><a style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;" href="#sAB171F94BB68041C84745A8C8894EBF7"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Table of Contents</font></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Denbury Resources Inc.</font></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">From inception through </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we have </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">recovered all our costs</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in this field, and our tertiary operations at Tinsley Field have generated excess net cash flow (revenue less operating expenses and capital expenditures, including the acquisition costs) of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$340 million</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, the estimated PV-10 Value of Tinsley Field was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">$1.0 billion</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Future Tertiary Properties with No Tertiary Production or Proved Tertiary Reserves at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:italic;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Webster Field.  </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">We acquired our interest in Webster Field in the fourth quarter of 2012 as part of the Bakken Exchange Transaction.  The field is located in Texas, approximately eight miles northeast of our Hastings Field, which we are currently flooding with CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  At December&#160;31, 2013, Webster Field had estimated proved non-tertiary reserves of approximately 3.2 MMBOE, net to our acquired interest.&#160;&#160;During the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, non-tertiary production at Webster Field averaged 1,036 BOE/d.&#160;&#160;Webster Field is geologically similar to our Hastings Field, producing oil from the Frio zone at similar depths; as a result, we believe it is well suited for CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR.  In </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $105 million to drill or recomplete injection and production wells and begin water injections to re-pressurize the reservoir.  We currently expect to commence CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections at Webster Field in 2015, with first tertiary production expected late that same year.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Conroe Field.&#160;&#160;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Conroe Field, our largest potential tertiary flood in the Gulf Coast region, is located north of Houston, Texas.&#160;&#160;We acquired a majority interest in this field in 2009 for $271 million in cash and 11.6 million shares of Denbury common stock, for a total aggregate value of $439 million.&#160;&#160;Conroe Field had estimated proved non-tertiary reserves of approximately 12.3 MMBOE at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, net to our interest, nearly all of which are proved developed.&#160;&#160;During the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, production at Conroe Field averaged 2,697 BOE/d, compared to 2,745 BOE/d in the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;&#160;Given the size of the Conroe Field (approximately 20,000 acres), the volume of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;that could be injected is quite sizable, and much larger than any field we have developed to date.&#160;&#160;Therefore, the pace of development will be dictated in part by the amount of available CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">A pipeline must be constructed so that CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;can be delivered to Conroe Field.&#160;&#160;This pipeline, which is planned as an extension of our Green Pipeline, is preliminarily estimated to cover approximately 90 miles at a cost of approximately $220 million.  We currently expect to begin construction of this pipeline in 2016 and to commence CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections at Conroe Field in 2017, with first tertiary production currently expected in 2018.  In </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to continue work on pipeline route selection, right-of-way acquisition, engineering, and regulatory permits while building our CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR development plan for Conroe Field.  In 2014, we also plan to invest approximately $30 million on non-tertiary well recompletions and to begin water injections into the area of the field in which we plan to commence CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections to begin building reservoir pressure.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Thompson Field.  </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">We acquired our interest in Thompson Field in June 2012 for $366 million.  The field is located in Texas, approximately 18 miles west of our Hastings Field.  Thompson Field had estimated proved non-tertiary reserves of approximately 15.4 MMBOE at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, net to our interest, of which approximately 54% are proved developed.&#160;&#160;During the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, non-tertiary production at Thompson Field averaged 1,331 BOE/d net to our interest, compared to 1,517 BOE/d in the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">2012</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.&#160; Thompson Field is geologically similar to Hastings Field, producing oil from the Frio zone at similar depths and we therefore believe it is well suited for CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR.  Under the terms of the Thompson Field acquisition agreement, after the initiation of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injection, the seller will retain approximately a 5% gross revenue interest (less severance taxes) once average monthly oil production exceeds 3,000 Bbls/d.  In </font><font style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $15 million on non-tertiary drilling opportunities and facility upgrades.  We currently plan to commence CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections at Thompson Field in 2018, with first tertiary production expected in 2020.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Rocky Mountain Region</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:7pt;font-style:italic;">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Sources and Pipelines</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">LaBarge Field.&#160;&#160;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">We acquired an overriding royalty interest equivalent to an approximate one-third ownership interest in ExxonMobil's CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves in LaBarge Field in the fourth quarter of 2012 as part of the Bakken Exchange Transaction.  LaBarge Field is located in southwestern Wyoming.  The gas composition from LaBarge Field is expected to be approximately 65% CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, approximately 18% to 20% methane, less than one percent helium, and the remainder various other gases.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">During December 2013, we received approximately 41 MMcf/d from ExxonMobil's Shute Creek gas processing plant at LaBarge Field.  Based on current capacity, and subject to availability of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we currently expect to ultimately receive up to 65 MMcf/d of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in 2014, rising to approximately 115 MMcf/d of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;by 2021 from such plant.  We pay ExxonMobil a fee to </font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">- </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;13</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;-</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><a style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;" href="#sAB171F94BB68041C84745A8C8894EBF7"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Table of Contents</font></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Denbury Resources Inc.</font></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">process and deliver the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, which we plan to use in our Rocky Mountain region CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;floods.  As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, our interest in LaBarge Field consisted of approximately </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">1.3</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Tcf of proved CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">The Riley Ridge Federal Unit is also located in southwestern Wyoming and produces gas from the same LaBarge Field.  In a series of two acquisitions in 2010 and 2011, we acquired 100% of the operating interests in Riley Ridge for $347 million.&#160;&#160;These purchases included a gas processing facility that was under construction at the purchase dates to separate the helium and natural gas from the gas stream.&#160;&#160;We placed our gas processing facility at Riley Ridge into service in the fourth quarter of 2013.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:28px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, our interest in Riley Ridge and minor surrounding acreage contained net proved reserves of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">399</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bcf (</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">67 MMBOE</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">) of natural gas and 2.0 Tcf of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves.&#160;&#160;The CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserve estimates are based on the gross working interest of the CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;reserves, in which our net revenue interest is approximately </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">1.6</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Tcf.&#160;&#160;The helium reserves at Riley Ridge are owned primarily by the U.S. government; however, we have the right to produce and sell the helium reserves to a third party on behalf of the government.  In exchange for this right, we pay the U.S. government a fee that fluctuates based upon realized sales proceeds.&#160;&#160;Our helium extraction agreement with the U.S. government has a minimum term extending 20 years from first production and continuing thereafter until either party terminates the contract.  Reserve volumes presented herein assume that the term of this helium extraction agreement continues beyond 20 years, given the benefit to both parties to the agreement.  As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we estimate that Riley Ridge contains proved helium reserves of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">13.3</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bcf, which volume estimate is reduced to reflect the related fee we will remit to the U.S. government.&#160;&#160;In addition, we believe there is significant reserve potential in other acreage surrounding Riley Ridge in which we also own an interest.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">The gas processing facility at Riley Ridge will separate for sale the natural gas and helium from the full well stream, and the remaining gases, including CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, will be re-injected into the producing formation or a deeper formation until we complete construction of a planned CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;capture facility and pipeline later this decade.&#160;&#160;We currently project that we will start to use CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from Riley Ridge around 2020, following completion of the capture facility and planned CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline connecting Riley Ridge to our existing Greencore Pipeline.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Other Rocky Mountain CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Sources.</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;We began purchasing and receiving CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from the Lost Cabin plant in central Wyoming in the first quarter of 2013, under a contract that provides us as much as 50 MMcf/d of CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;for use in our Rocky Mountain region CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;floods.  Our volumes received from the plant averaged approximately 22 MMcf/d in 2013. &#160;We plan to continue to pursue additional sources for CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;supply in the Rocky Mountain region.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Greencore Pipeline.&#160;&#160;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">The 20-inch Greencore Pipeline in Wyoming is the first CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline we have constructed in the Rocky Mountain region.&#160;&#160;We plan to use the pipeline as our trunk line in the Rocky Mountain region, eventually connecting our Lost Cabin, LaBarge and Riley Ridge CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;sources (see </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Rocky Mountain Region CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Sources and Pipelines</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;above) to the Cedar Creek Anticline in eastern Montana.  The initial 232-mile section of the Greencore Pipeline begins at the Lost Cabin gas plant in Wyoming and terminates at Bell Creek Field in Montana.&#160;&#160;We completed construction of this section of the pipeline in the fourth quarter of 2012 and received our first CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;deliveries from the Lost Cabin gas plant during the first quarter of 2013.&#160;&#160;In the first quarter of 2014, we completed construction of an interconnect between our Greencore Pipeline and an existing third-party CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline in Wyoming, which will enable us to transport CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;from LaBarge Field to our Bell Creek Field.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Tertiary Properties with Tertiary Production and Proved Tertiary Reserves at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Bell Creek Field.&#160;&#160;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Bell Creek Field is located in southeast Montana.&#160;&#160;The oil-producing reservoir in Bell Creek Field is a sandstone reservoir with characteristics similar to those we have successfully flooded with CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;in the Gulf Coast region; as a result, we believe it is well suited for CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub We acquired our interest in Bell Creek Field through the Encore merger in 2010 and have worked since that time to commence a CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR project in the field.  We began first CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections during the second quarter of 2013, recorded our first tertiary oil production in the third quarter of 2013, and booked initial proved tertiary reserves in the fourth quarter of 2013.  Tertiary production, net to our interest, during the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;averaged </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">177</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bbls/d.&#160;&#160;In </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $55 million to expand our CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;flood of Bell Creek Field.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">From inception through </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we had </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">not yet recovered</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;our investment in this field, and the remaining investment to be recovered (revenue less operating expenses and capital expenditures, including the acquisition costs) from Bell Creek Field was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">$432 million</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  As of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, the estimated PV-10 Value of Bell Creek Field was </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;text-decoration:none;">$739.0 million</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  </font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div Inc.</font></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Future Tertiary Properties with No Tertiary Production or Proved Tertiary Reserves at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:italic;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font we own and currently our largest producing property.  The field is primarily located in Montana but covers such a large area (approximately 126 miles) that it also extends into North Dakota.&#160;&#160;CCA is a series of 14 producing areas, each of which could be considered a field by itself.&#160;&#160;We acquired our initial interest in CCA as part of the Encore merger in 2010 and acquired additional interests (the "CCA Acquisition") from a wholly-owned subsidiary of ConocoPhillips in the first quarter of 2013.  See </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">2013 Business Developments</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;above and </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">Note 2</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:italic;text-decoration:none;">Acquisitions and Divestitures</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, to the Consolidated Financial Statements for further discussion of this transaction and information as to other recent acquisitions and divestitures by Denbury.  The 2013 CCA Acquisition added </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">42.2</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;MMBOE of incremental proved reserves.  Production from CCA, net to our interest, averaged </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">18,601</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;BOE/d during the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, compared to pro forma production during the fourth quarter of 2012 of 19,493 BOE/d (including production associated with our newly acquired CCA assets of approximately 11,000 BOE/d and production from our previously owned CCA assets of 8,493 BOE/d).  The non-tertiary proved reserves associated with CCA were </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">105.4</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;MMBbls of oil and </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">6.0</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Bcf of gas as of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">CCA is located approximately 110 miles north of Bell Creek Field, and we currently expect to ultimately connect this field to our Greencore Pipeline.&#160;&#160;In 2014, we plan to invest approximately $110 million to improve waterfloods, drill new wells, and recomplete existing wells.  We currently plan to commence CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font agreement, under which we will obtain a 65% working interest in Grieve Field, located in Natrona County, Wyoming, in exchange for developing the Grieve Field CO</font><font completed a three-mile CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font Grieve Field in the fourth quarter of 2012, and are preparing for construction of the field's CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub during the first quarter of 2013 and currently expect tertiary production to commence in 2015.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font The field is located in the Powder River Basin of northeastern Wyoming, approximately 12 miles from our Greencore Pipeline.  Hartzog Draw Field had estimated proved reserves of approximately 5.2 MMBOE at </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;">December&#160;31, 2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, net to our acquired interest, 1.9 MMBOE of which relate to the natural gas producing Big George coal zone.&#160;&#160;During the </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">fourth quarter</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, non-tertiary production averaged 2,204 BOE/d.  We believe the oil reservoir characteristics of Hartzog Draw Field make it well suited for CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR.  In </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2014</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, we plan to invest approximately $40 million to drill and complete six horizontal wells in the Shannon formation and re-frac eight existing wells.  We anticipate drilling additional horizontal wells in the Shannon formation over the next several years.  The drilling of these wells is expected to generate near-term cash flow, as well as complement our planned future CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR project in the field.  We must obtain regulatory approval and construct a CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;pipeline from our existing Greencore Pipeline to Hartzog Draw Field before we can commence our planned CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;EOR project.  We currently plan to commence CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;injections at Hartzog Draw Field after 2020.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Other Non-Tertiary Oil Properties</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Although almost all of our oil and natural gas properties are either existing or planned future tertiary floods (discussed above), we also produce oil and natural gas either from fields that are not amenable to EOR or out of specific reservoirs (within an existing tertiary field) that are not amenable to EOR.  For example, at Heidelberg Field, we produce natural gas from the Selma Chalk reservoir, which is separate from the Christmas and Eutaw reservoirs currently being flooded with CO</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><sub style="vertical-align:bottom;line-height:120%;font-size:7pt">2</sub></font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">.  Production from these other non-tertiary properties totaled 6,994 BOE/d during the fourth quarter of </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;color:#000000;text-decoration:none;">2013</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">, compared to 18,615 BOE/d during the fourth quarter of 2012.  Production during the fourth quarter of 2012 includes 10,064 BOE/d of production from our Bakken area assets that were sold during that period.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">&#160;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">OIL AND GAS ACREAGE, PRODUCTIVE WELLS AND DRILLING ACTIVITY</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">In the data below, "gross" represents the total acres or wells in which we own a working interest and "net" represents the gross acres or wells multiplied by our working interest percentage.&#160;&#160;For the wells that produce both oil and gas, the well is typically classified as an oil or natural gas well based on the ratio of oil to natural gas production.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;text-indent:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font </font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;15</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;-</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div cellspacing="0" style="font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:24px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:0px;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">(1)</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">An exploratory well is a well drilled to find a new field or to find a new reservoir in a field previously found to be productive of oil or natural gas in another reservoir.&#160; Generally, an exploratory well is any well that is not a development well, an extension well, a service well or a stratigraphic test well.&#160; A development well is a well drilled within the proved area of an oil or gas reservoir to the depth of a stratigraphic horizon known to be productive.</font></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;padding-left:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><table style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">A productive well is an exploratory or development well found to be capable of producing either oil or natural gas in sufficient quantities to justify completion as an oil or natural gas well.</font></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;padding-left:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" well.</font></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:justify;padding-left:24px;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><table
